active_goal: Improve MemoryOS Lite v3 into a benchmark-usable Letta-style agent memory system for LongMemEval and LoCoMo, without demo-only phase completion, without hiding case-level regressions, and without enabling the v3 kernel by default.

context_bundle.summary: Phase 16 K3 kernel maintenance tool surface planning bundle: preserve v3 default, v1 fallback, kernel opt-in, K2 selection boundary, approval replay binding, service-backed maintenance tools, no benchmark gold leakage, structural smoke only.

current_phase_objective: Plan the smallest usable K3 maintenance tool surface for the opt-in v3 kernel so Phase 17 can run LoCoMo repair smoke without demo-only tool wiring or hidden regressions.

phase_bootstrap_decision.reason: state.json was in GOD_DISPATCH and phase-16 context/dispatch/plan artifacts were missing; bootstrap rules allow only missing phase-local dispatch and planning artifacts in this run.

failing_tests_required_before_implementa: Add RED tests proving archive_attach requires source refs or approval, routes through a named service, verifies ArchiveAttachment plus v3 eligibility, and denies replay tampering before execution.
Add RED tests proving core_promotion_request creates only a pending PromotionCandidate through MemoryLifecycleService and never mutates core memory or v3 core context during Phase 16.
If recall_search/archive_search are opened, add RED tests proving they are read-only, bounded, traced, and do not write messages/memory except controlled tool result messages.
Add RED tests proving core_memory_append, core_memory_replace, destructive delete/deprecate, and unknown tools remain fail-closed before policy/execution.
Add public benchmark guards proving kernel_trace_events remain empty by default and opt-in structural smoke emits only scoped tool events.

focused: uv run pytest tests/test_memory_lifecycle.py tests/test_core_memory_service.py tests/test_archival_store.py tests/test_context_composer.py -q
benchmark_and_smoke_commands.opt_in_structural_smoke: MEMORYOS_MEMORY_ARCH=v3 MEMORYOS_AGENT_KERNEL=v1 uv run memoryos eval public --benchmark locomo --data-path benchmarks/locomo/locomo10.json --baseline memoryos_lite --limit 5 --no-llm-answer --no-llm-judge --run-id phase16_locomo5_kernel_tools_structural
benchmark_and_smoke_commands.default_off_guard: MEMORYOS_MEMORY_ARCH=v3 uv run memoryos eval public --benchmark locomo --data-path benchmarks/locomo/locomo10.json --baseline memoryos_lite --limit 5 --no-llm-answer --no-llm-judge --run-id phase16_locomo5_kernel_default_off_guard

usable_ack_checklist: All plan/execute/review artifacts cite work/phase-16/context_bundle.md and the active goal.
Each opened tool has registry, candidate generation, policy, approval semantics if mutating, named executor/service, post-action verification, trace, and integration coverage.
Mutating tools require source refs or approved approval provenance.
Replay tampering cannot execute or verify a write.
Unsupported, Level 3, and destructive tools fail closed.
Maintenance writes are visible to v3 only through scope/provenance rules.
Default kernel-off public reports remain unchanged.
Phase 16 reports structural smoke separately from benchmark-quality evidence and makes no LongMemEval-only or aggregate improvement claim.
review_focus: Anti-demo gate: reject registry-only or happy-path-only tools.
LoCoMo-specific safety: repair artifacts must not use case ids, expected answers, expected source ids, judge labels, or gold-derived failure classes.
Source grounding: every write tool must have source refs or approved approval provenance.
Replay safety: approval_id, tool_call_id, and request fingerprint must stay binding for new tools.
Service boundary: no new tool may bypass MemoryLifecycleService, CoreMemoryService, or named archive/retrieval maintenance service with ad hoc store writes.
Default behavior: v3 default, v1 fallback, and kernel opt-in must remain unchanged.
Eval routing: Phase 16 structural smoke can support ACK, not benchmark-quality promotion.
